data.title: UK Mandates Crypto Exchanges to Collect Tax Data by 2026
data.description:

data.title: CoinShares Cancels Plans for Three Cryptocurrency ETFs in the U.S.
data.description: According to PANews, CoinShares has decided to halt its plans to launch three single-asset cryptocurrency ETFs in the United States. The canceled funds were intended to include Solana, featuring staking capabilities, as well as XRP and Litecoin.

data.title: São Paulo Launches Blockchain Microloan Program for Small Farmers in 2025
data.description:
São Paulo’s city government is preparing to roll out a blockchain-powered microloan system aimed at supporting small rural producers, with the pilot scheduled to go live in December 2025. The initiative is designed to simplify access to credit for farmers who often face barriers in Brazil’s traditional banking system.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/sao-paulo-launches-blockchain-microloan-program-for-small-farmers-in-2025/

data.title: Bitcoin hasn’t seen this much ‘asymmetric risk-reward’ since COVID: Analyst
data.description:
Bitwise crypto researcher André Dragosch said, “We’re staring at a similar macro setup” for Bitcoin as during the COVID-19 pandemic.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-price-risk-reward-covid-19-setup-analyst?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: UK Mandates Crypto Exchanges to Report Customer Transactions Starting 2026
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, the UK tax authority, HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC), has announced new regulations requiring cryptocurrency exchanges operating in the UK to collect comprehensive transaction records of all their UK customers starting January 1, 2026. These records must be shared with HMRC the following year. The collected data will be used to verify tax returns, ensure compliance, and penalize any violations.

The new guidelines align the UK with the OECD's Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework (CARF), which aims to enhance transparency in the rapidly growing digital asset market. This framework is already being implemented in regions such as the European Union, Canada, Australia, Japan, and South Korea.

Sui […] The post $1

data.title: Amundi launches tokenized euro money market fund on Ethereum
data.description: Amundi, Europe's largest asset manager, has launched its first tokenized share class for a euro money market fund. The fund is now offered in a hybrid structure, allowing investors to choose between a traditional version and a new blockchain-based version. The first transaction was recorded on the Ethereum network on November 4th. This product launch was accomplished in partnership with European asset services group CACEIS, which provided the tokenization infrastructure, investor wallets, and a digital order system for processing subscriptions and redemptions. According to the two companies, tokenizing the fund simplifies order processing, expands investor reach, and enables 24/7 trading. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bitwise has updated its filing for the spot Avalanche ETF, proposing to add staking functionality.
data.description: Bitwise has updated its filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for its spot Avalanche ETF. The revision changes the ETF's ticker symbol to BAVA and sets a sponsorship fee rate of 0.34%, currently the lowest among similar products. In comparison, VanEck's Avalanche ETF has a fee rate of 0.40%, and Grayscale's is 0.50%. The updated S-1 filing also states that the trust will be allowed to stake up to 70% of its AVAX holdings on Avalanche's proof-of-stake network to earn additional tokens. However, the issuer is considering deducting 12% of the proceeds as fees, with the remainder distributed to shareholders. Since competitors have not yet launched staking services, their fees are currently limited to sponsorship fees. Bitwise is also offering a full fee waiver for the first month on its initial $500 million in assets, aiming to position BAVA as the lowest-cost way for traditional investors to gain exposure to Avalanche and earn staking income. (CoinDesk)

data.title: The Balancer community has proposed a plan to allocate funds recovered from the hacking incident.
data.description: Two members of the Balancer protocol community submitted a proposal on Thursday outlining a distribution plan for a portion of the funds recovered from the $116 million attack that occurred on the protocol in November. Of the $116 million theft, approximately $28 million has already been recovered by white-hat hackers, internal rescue teams, and the Ethereum liquidity staking platform StakeWise. However, the proposal only covers the $8 million recovered by white-hat hackers and the internal rescue team; the nearly $20 million recovered by StakeWise will be distributed separately to its users. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Institutional Buyers Now Absorb Nearly All New Bitcoin Supply, Data Shows
data.description:
New data from Bitwise shows institutional appetite for Bitcoin has surged to one of the strongest levels in years, with demand now far exceeding newly issued supply. The chart highlights a structural shift: institutions are accumulating Bitcoin at a pace that the network’s issuance can no longer match.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/institutional-buyers-now-absorb-nearly-all-new-bitcoin-supply-data-shows/

data.title: CME Blackout: Traders Cry “Manipulation” After 10-Hour Halt Freezes Markets
data.description:
CME has faced a rare outage after a data center cooling failure has halted Globex trading for hours, disrupting futures in equities, rates, commodities and crypto as traders have questioned single-point risks and thin Thanksgiving liquidity.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/cme-blackout-traders-cry-manipulation-after-10-hour-halt-freezes-markets/
